1_thailand_applicability.rationale: Three Thai government sources converge: DOAE ESC names the five primary Thai production provinces (Chiang Rai/Phayao/Loei/Phetchabun/Si Sa Ket), DOAE Agriman supplies multi-year national production statistics 2563-2565, and DOA OARD2 documents the bacterial-wilt-driven 'soil sequence problem' that defines the Thai applicability story.

2_climate.rationale: DOAE ESC supplies the Thai 80-100 inch rainfall figure and 0-1500m elevation envelope, CTAHR confirms similar 125-250 inches in Hilo, DOA OARD2 specifies February-April planting window, and UF/IFAS adds shade requirement (25-40%) for hot-summer adaptation.

3_soil.rationale: DOAE ESC sets the Thai pH 6.0-6.5 standard, CTAHR confirms 5.5-6.5, DOA OARD2 specifies the urea+lime soil-disinfection protocol (80:800 kg/rai), and the Chiang Rai fertilizer trial gives actual soil-test values (pH 5.9, OM 3.95%, P 14, K 296 mg/kg) — four-source convergence.

4_water.rationale: OPSMOAC Samut Sakhon documents the 2-3x daily watering practice for small farmers, DOA OARD2 emphasizes the 5-7 day waterlogging-disease cascade that defines water-management risk, and the Chiang Rai trial confirms the 1-month pre-harvest dry-down protocol.

5_planting.rationale: DOA OARD2 supplies seed-rhizome cutting (2-3 inches, 3-4 eyes), Bacillus subtilis BS-DOA 24 dip protocol (50g/20L, 30 min), and 25-30 cm in-row spacing; DOAE ESC defines mature seed selection (10-20 months); OPSMOAC documents the practical 8-10 cm planting hole; the harvest-age trial confirms tissue-culture G0 protocol; KU thesis 1997 is the foundational tissue-culture reference.

6_care.rationale: Chiang Rai trial (3-year RCB experiment) provides definitive NPK rates with statistically-significant 8,947 kg/rai yield and 46% fertilizer-cost reduction; DOA OARD2 supplies sanitation/quarantine protocol; OPSMOAC supplies weeding intervals (1, 2, 3 months). High confidence — multiple Thai government experimental sources.

7_pests_diseases.rationale: DOA OARD2 is the canonical Thai source on Ralstonia solanacearum management for ginger; CTAHR SCM-8 supplies the famous 1993 45% statewide loss figure that anchors disease severity; CTAHR CFS-GIN-3A enumerates Hawaiian-context disease/pest list; UF/IFAS adds Pythium/Phytophthora and rotation interval (3-5 yr). Multi-source convergence on the dominant disease story.

8_harvest.rationale: FAO 2002 supplies the canonical timing matrix (5 mo fresh / 5-7 mo preserve / 8-9 mo dried/oil) and post-harvest curing/drying protocol; OPSMOAC adds the Thai practical 4-6 month young-ginger window (every 12-15 days); Chiang Rai trial confirms 11-month mature harvest; CTAHR confirms 3-5 day curing; DOAE Agriman supplies actual 2565 monthly market prices.

9_economics.rationale: DOAE Agriman provides the official cost breakdown (45,700 baht/rai, 6,000 kg yield, 14,300 baht profit) and three years of national production statistics; Loei competitive analysis supplies the TCM Question Mark positioning and value-chain critique; Chiang Rai fertilizer trial demonstrates that better fertilizer technology can add 13,103-20,233 baht/rai. All three are Thai government sources with explicit numbers.

10_thailand_notes.rationale: DOAE ESC names the five primary provinces; Chiang Rai trial documents Northern (highland) production; Loei competitive analysis documents the Northeast (Phu Ruea/Na Haeo) specifics; DOAE Agriman supplies national 2565 export 98,806 tonnes / 1,980.5 MB import figures. All four are Thai government sources giving the regional and market story.

11_foreign_knowledge.rationale: CTAHR CFS-GIN-3A supplies Hawaiian production figures (40,000 lb/acre); SCM-8 supplies non-circulating hydroponic system specs; UF/IFAS supplies Florida shade/photoperiod data; FAO supplies world production trajectory and trade flows; KU 1997 thesis represents the foundational Thai academic linkage. Five sources, all high confidence; explicit Thailand-applicability note included for each.

overall_rationale: All eleven sections rest on at least three independently converging high-confidence sources, with the bacterial-wilt management story (sections 1, 5, 7) anchored by both Thai DOA primary research and CTAHR Hawaii's well-documented 1993 outbreak. Economic data is unusually well-sourced for a Thai crop profile because DOAE Agriman publishes a complete cost-revenue breakdown with multi-year statistics. The only reservation: market price figures and competitive position carry inherent volatility, but the underlying methodology and source traceability are high-quality.
